categories.name: Home
categories.prompt:
You judge the homepage of a specialty coffee roaster.

A visitor wants to answer: "What do they sell? Are they legit? Should I buy here?"

Good page:
- Hero shows the product (bags of coffee, roasting process)
- Clear value proposition within 3 seconds
- Navigation to shop, subscriptions, about
- Social proof (reviews, press mentions, awards)
- Fresh content signals (seasonal blends, recent roast dates)

Bad page:
- Generic stock photos of coffee cups
- Long brand story before showing any product
- No clear call to action
- Looks like a template with placeholder content

SEO check:
- Title includes brand + "specialty coffee" or "coffee roasters"
- H1 matches
- Product schema or Organization schema
- Fast load, optimized images

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of the homepage>",
  "conversionScore": "<1-10, does the homepage make a visitor want to buy?>",
  "seoScore": "<1-10, can Google find and rank this page?>",
  "valuePropositionClear": true/false,
  "shopLinkVisible": true/false,
  "socialProofPresent": true/false,
  "freshContentSignals": true/false,
  "notes": "<one sentence assessment>"
}

categories.name: Shop
categories.extraInfo: online store, buy coffee, coffee beans, blends, single origin, product catalog
categories.prompt:
You judge the online shop / product listing of a coffee roaster.

A visitor wants to: browse, compare, and buy coffee beans.

Good page:
- Products visible with images, names, prices
- Flavor notes or tasting profiles shown per product
- Roast level, origin, process visible without clicking
- Filter/sort by origin, roast, flavor, price
- Clear add-to-cart flow
- Roast date or freshness guarantee mentioned

Bad page:
- Just names and prices, no tasting info
- Must click into each product to see details
- No filtering on a large catalog
- Confusing subscription vs one-time options
- Out of stock items shown without indication

SEO check:
- Category page titles include "buy coffee beans online" or similar
- Product schema with price, availability, reviews
- Breadcrumbs
- Unique descriptions, not copy-pasted from importers

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of the shop page>",
  "conversionScore": "<1-10, how easy is it to find and buy the right coffee?>",
  "seoScore": "<1-10, how well can Google index and rank these products?>",
  "flavorNotesVisible": true/false,
  "filteringAvailable": true/false,
  "roastDateVisible": true/false,
  "notes": "<one sentence assessment>"
}

categories.name: Subscription
categories.extraInfo: coffee subscription, recurring delivery, subscription box, monthly coffee
categories.prompt:
You judge coffee subscription pages.

A visitor wants to answer: "How much? How often? Can I customize? Can I cancel?"

Good page:
- Pricing clear: per bag, per month
- Frequency options visible (weekly, biweekly, monthly)
- Customization: roast preference, grind, quantity
- Easy cancellation policy stated
- What makes this subscription special (curated, seasonal, single origin rotation)

Bad page:
- Price hidden behind "get started"
- No explanation of what you'll receive
- Unclear commitment or cancellation terms
- Just a generic "subscribe and save" with no personality

SEO check:
- Title includes "coffee subscription" + brand
- FAQ schema for common questions
- Reviews or testimonials

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of the subscription page>",
  "conversionScore": "<1-10, does it make subscribing easy and appealing?>",
  "seoScore": "<1-10, can Google find this for coffee subscription queries?>",
  "pricingClear": true/false,
  "frequencyOptions": true/false,
  "cancellationPolicyClear": true/false,
  "notes": "<one sentence assessment>"
}

categories.name: About
categories.extraInfo: about us, our story, the roastery, team, sourcing, sustainability, direct trade
categories.prompt:
You judge the about / story page of a coffee roaster.

A visitor wants to know: Who are these people? Where do they source? What's their approach?

Good page:
- Real people with names and photos
- Sourcing philosophy (direct trade, relationships, transparency)
- Roasting approach explained simply
- Location and roastery shown
- Sustainability or ethical practices mentioned with specifics

Bad page:
- Generic "we love coffee" without substance
- No real team info
- Claims about quality with no evidence
- Wall of text with no structure

SEO check:
- Title includes brand + "about" or "our story"
- Structured content with headers
- Images with alt text

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of the about page>",
  "conversionScore": "<1-10, does the story build trust and make you want to buy?>",
  "seoScore": "<1-10, does the page rank for branded queries?>",
  "teamVisible": true/false,
  "sourcingPhilosophyVisible": true/false,
  "notes": "<one sentence assessment>"
}

categories.name: Contact
categories.extraInfo: contact page, visit the roastery, location, cafe
categories.prompt:
You judge contact pages for a coffee roaster. A visitor needs: address, email, phone, cafe hours (if applicable), wholesale inquiry option. Good pages show all of this above the fold with a map.

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of the page>",
  "conversionScore": "<1-10, clear path to reach the business or visit>",
  "seoScore": "<1-10, LocalBusiness schema, correct NAP, unique copy>",
  "hasAddress": true/false,
  "hasPhone": true/false,
  "hasEmail": true/false,
  "hasMap": true/false,
  "hasOpeningHours": true/false,
  "notes": "<one sentence assessment>"
}
